strategy: Preserve the transcript-grounded room phrase, then use a single reinterpretation step over prior evidence plus the relevant preprocess transcripts to test whether any answer option can be justified from in-video evidence alone and to explicitly report if the founder death year remains ungrounded.

steps.purpose: Re-evaluate the question using the preserved prior evidence and the relevant preprocess transcripts, extracting the exact room-description year phrase, checking whether the founder's death year is stated anywhere in the available transcript coverage, and determining whether any option is supportable without outside knowledge.

inputs.query: Using only the provided transcripts and prior evidence, do all of the following: (1) quote the exact phrase the narrator uses to describe the rooms/bedrooms and state whether it is a specific year or only a decade phrase; (2) quote every transcript mention of Ed Sheflin / Tombstone founder and state whether any mention gives his death year; (3) if and only if two grounded numeric years are available, compute the sum and map it to the answer option; otherwise explicitly say that no option is justified from the video evidence alone. Do not use outside knowledge. Treat '1880s flair' as a decade phrase unless the transcript itself provides a rule to normalize it to a single year.
text_contexts: Audit gaps to resolve: the town founder's death year grounded by the provided evidence; how the narrator's bedroom description phrase '1880s flair' should be converted into a single year for the requested sum, if at all; a justified mapping from grounded values to one answer option.
Prior trace claim: The narrator's year phrase for the rooms/bedrooms is explicitly 'decorated in an 1880s flair.'
Prior trace claim: The transcript identifies Ed Sheflin as Tombstone's founder and discusses him in later narration, but none of the provided transcript spans explicitly states his death year.

segments.text: This most unique member of the Dude Ranchers Association is laid out to look just like a town out of the Old West. But behind the doors of what appears to be the mining office, Miss Kitty's bordello, or Doc Holliday's dentist chair, are state-of-the-art rooms, decorated in an 1880s flair. We live in the newspaper. Juergen von Wolf and his wife Karen were here from Germany on a springtime visit to the ranch, their seventh in just five years, with plans for two more trips by the end of the year.

That famous town is just two miles away. We'll take you for a visit in just a bit. But even closer stands the namesake of Tombstone Monument Ranch, the 25-foot tall stone monument to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin. The Apaches ruled this country when the prospector came here looking for gold. Friends warned all he'd find in the desert would be his own tombstone.

And he came close one day when he hid out from an Apache war party in some rocks where that monument now stands, which you can visit on a trail ride. But when Sheflin discovered the silver strike that made him rich, he had the last laugh, naming his new town Tombstone, which would prove to be a fitting name. Here lies George Johnson, hanged by mistake, 1882. He was right, we was wrong, but we strung him up, and now he's gone.

expected_outputs.answer: A determination of whether any option is justified from in-video evidence alone, or an explicit statement that none is justified
expected_outputs.supporting_points: Quoted transcript evidence for the room phrase and founder mentions, plus whether the death year is absent
expected_outputs.analysis: Explicit reasoning about why '1880s flair' is or is not normalizable to a single year and whether option mapping is possible

refinement_instructions: Preserve the existing grounded transcript facts: the room description is 'decorated in an 1880s flair,' and Ed Sheflin is identified as Tombstone founder. Do not introduce outside knowledge for Ed Sheflin's death year. In the trace, explicitly distinguish founder mention from founder death-year evidence, and explicitly distinguish a decade phrase ('1880s') from a single numeric year. If step 1 confirms that no transcript-grounded death year exists and no valid normalization rule turns '1880s' into one specific year, the trace should state that no answer option is justified from the video evidence alone rather than fabricating a sum. If step 1 somehow finds two grounded numeric years within the provided evidence, then compute the sum and map it to the option using those quoted values only.
